item.title: Watch Live: Senate holds "vote-a-rama" on ICE funding ahead of final passage
item.description: The Senate is holding what's expected to be a marathon vote series as Republicans seek to fund immigration agencies under the Department of Homeland Security without help from Democrats.

item.title: Mullin threatens to pull customs officers from sanctuary cities days before World Cup
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin faced lawmakers' questions on his threat to pull U.S. Customs and Border Patrol agents from airports in sanctuary cities, a move that could impact travelers for the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Expert reacts to Trump's latest definition of a "ceasefire" in the Middle East
item.description: Retired U.S. Army major and military analyst Mike Lyons joins CBS News Mornings with his take on President Trump's latest definition of a "ceasefire" in Iran after retaliatory strikes in Kuwait turned deadly. Mr. Trump said that in the Middle East, a "ceasefire is when you're shooting in a more moderate manner."

item.title: 1 dead, 3 hurt in high school graduation shooting in Northern California
item.description: One person died and three others, including a child, were injured in a shooting Wednesday night at a high school graduation in Northern California, officials said.

item.title: Iran says "no tangible progress" in talks; Hezbollah rejects Israel-Lebanon truce
item.description: Iran says there's been no progress in talks with the U.S. after tit-for-tat strikes, as Hezbollah rejects new Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

item.title: What are today's HELOC and home equity loan interest rates?
item.description: HELOCs and home equity loans offer homeowners an affordable way to borrow money now. Here are the rates for each.

item.title: What are today's mortgage and mortgage refinance interest rates?
item.description: Knowing today's mortgage rates can help new homebuyers and homeowners looking to lock in a good deal.

item.title: What is the price of silver today?
item.description: Silver can play a critical role in a diversified portfolio. Here's how much it costs per ounce right now.

item.title: Strikes reportedly continue despite Israel-Lebanon truce, House rebukes Trump on Iran
item.description: Israel and Lebanon renewed a ceasefire agreement despite reports of ongoing strikes exchanged. This comes as American lawmakers weigh reining in President Trump's powers on Iran. CBS News' Taurean Small reports.

item.title: Family of Auburn student missing in Japan thinks he was going hiking before he disappeared
item.description: A search is underway for James "Weston" Higginbotham, a 20-year-old Auburn student who went missing on a family trip to Japan. His parents believe their son was headed to a hiking trail before disappearing. Anna Coren reports.

item.title: Trump expected to nominate Todd Blanche as permanent attorney general
item.description: President Trump is expected to nominate Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to serve in the role permanently, several sources familiar with the matter told CBS News.

item.title: What is the price of gold today?
item.description: Understanding the pricing trends and dynamics of the gold market is crucial for making sound financial choices.

item.title: Trump expected to nominate Todd Blanche as attorney general after "anti-weaponization fund" fallout
item.description: President Trump is expected to nominate Todd Blanche, who served as his personal attorney before his second term in the White House, as U.S. attorney general. This comes despite intense backlash over a $1.776 billion "anti-weaponization fund" for people who allege they were victims of government persecution. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.

item.title: What's the mortgage interest rate forecast for summer 2026? Here's what some experts predict.
item.description: Before you start comparing your mortgage loan options, make sure you know where interest rates could head next.

item.title: Knicks overcome 14-point deficit to beat Spurs in Game 1 of NBA Finals
item.description: The New York Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs in Game 1 of the NBA Finals after erasing a 14-point deficit. Nate Burleson recaps the game.

item.title: She waited for a soulmate who never showed up: ChatGPT users detail AI delusions
item.description: AI-fueled delusions can happen when chatbots respond to grandiose, paranoid or imaginary ideas with affirmation or encouragement.

item.title: Nick Saban testifies in support of bill to regulate NIL rules: "It's become an arms race"
item.description: Retired Alabama football coach Nick Saban testified on Capitol Hill on Wednesday in support of legislation to regulate the NCAA's name, image and likeness rules. Kelly O'Grady reports.

item.title: Victim of "horrifying" Chelsea murder identified after 26 years
item.description: "Chelsea Jane Doe," who was found brutally murdered in Massachusetts in 2000, has been identified as Tiffany Bradley of Allentown, Pennsylvania.

item.title: Social Security checks could be cut by $500 a month in 2032, report finds
item.description: Beneficiaries would continue receiving payments if Social Security's trust fund is depleted, but checks could shrink by about 24%, according to a new report.

item.title: Guide missing for a week on Mount Everest found alive: "Miracle"
item.description: A Sherpa guide was found crawling to base camp on Mount Everest a week after he went missing.

item.title: Hostages safely rescued, suspect killed after nearly 16-hour standoff at California bank
item.description: After nearly 16 hours, all hostages being held inside a California bank were safely rescued and the suspect barricaded inside was killed by an elite FBI team, officials say. Jonathan Vigliotti has the latest.

item.title: Friend of missing American in Bahamas hopes to get answers: "It makes you sick"
item.description: A friend of Lynette Hooker, who went missing in the Bahamas two months ago, spoke to CBS News about Hooker's disappearance as a U.S. Coast Guard team continues its search. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Ford recalls nearly 420,000 vehicles over faulty seat belts
item.description: Safety watchdog said seat belts in certain Ford Expedition and Lincoln vehicles may inadvertently lock, preventing them from functioning properly.

item.title: NASA reluctantly gives up on lost orbiter: "Best Mars mission ever"
item.description: NASA officials said the $582 million MAVEN orbiter could not be recovered after a problem on the far side of Mars late last year, and that its extraordinarily successful mission was at an end.

item.title: Flesh-eating New World screwworm found in Texas calf, USDA says
item.description: The USDA said the only animal affected was a 3-week-old calf in Zavala County, Texas, after larvae were identified in its umbilical area.

item.title: U.S. destroys alleged drug boat in Pacific, killing 2 more people
item.description: At least 207 people have been killed since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls "narcoterrorists" in September.

item.title: SpaceX plans record stock market debut that could make Elon Musk a trillionaire
item.description: SpaceX says it plans to raise up to $75 billion when it goes public this month in what could be the largest stock market debut ever, and it would put Elon Musk on course to becoming the first trillionaire.

item.title: SPLC asks judge to weigh sanctions against DOJ for sending out unsigned indictment copy
item.description: The Southern Poverty Law Center asked a judge to consider sanctioning federal prosecutors, after the DOJ shared an unsigned and unstamped copy of a superseding indictment with members of the media.

item.title: California primary results for key races including governor, LA mayor
item.description: The races for governor and Los Angeles mayor are among the most closely watched contests yet to be decided in California's primary elections.

item.title: Rep. Ken Calvert advances in the Congressional CA-40 primary, CBS News projects
item.description: Calvert, who currently represents California's 41st Congressional District, announced his campaign​ shortly after California voters passed Proposition 50,​ which shifted five districts to be more favorable to Democrats in the midterm elections this year.
